channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Senate is preparing to confirm Hegseth as defense secretary in late evening vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is muscling Pete Hegseth's nomination as defense secretary toward confirmation Friday, prioritizing his vow to create a “warrior culture” at the Pentagon over allegations of heavy drinking and aggressive behavior toward women.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Oklahoma wants federal inmate transferred so he can be put to death
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma's top prosecutor asked the federal Bureau of Prisons to transfer an inmate to state custody so that he could be executed for his role in the kidnapping and killing of a 77-year-old woman in 1999.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump cuts Dr. Fauci's security detail
item.description: Asked if he would feel partially responsible if something were to happen to Fauci, Trump said he would not.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KREX 5

item.title: Marilyn Manson won't face domestic violence and sexual assault charges, DA says
item.description: "We recognize and applaud the courage and resilience of the women who came forward to make reports and share their experiences, and we thank them for their cooperation and patience with the investigation," the district attorney's office said in a news release explaining the decision not to pursue ch
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Spirit Airlines updates dress code, tattoo policy for passengers
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines can now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or other "body art" is deemed "lewd, obscene, or offensive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the ruling blocking Trump's order on birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally has faced the first of what will be many legal tests. It didn't fare well.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass' brother loses home in Palisades Fire
item.description: Embattled Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass has revealed that her brother lost his Malibu home in the still-raging Palisades wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Mariann Budde, the bishop who upset Trump with her inaugural sermon
item.description: The Right Rev. Mariann Budde, Episcopal bishop of the Diocese of Washington, made headlines this week after she angered President Donald Trump with her sermon during an inaugural prayer service.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Stanley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ford recalls over 270,000 Broncos and Mavericks: Here's why
item.description: Ford Motor Co. is recalling 272,827 Broncos and Mavericks due to a power problem that can cause vehicles to stop unexpectedly or fail to start.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: High school football player returns to gym after losing arm in ATV accident
item.description: "Being active again has been great for me. It's been great for my mental just getting back to the grind and the normal things I was doing before," Trooper Wright said.
item.dc:creator: Justin Lundy, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents’ immigration status.
item.dc:creator: EUGENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration blocks federal health agencies from communicating with public
item.description: The Trump administration has put a freeze on many federal health agency communications with the public through at least the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE and JONEL ALECCIA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: CNN to lay off dozens as part of major restructuring
item.description: Cable giant CNN is planning to lay off dozens of employees and undergo a major restructuring as the outlet looks to retool itself around digital during President Donald Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Samsung S25 Lineup: Lighter, Faster, More AI
item.description: Samsung held their latest unpacked event in the heart of Silicon Valley in San Jose. It's where AI is a major buzzword and that was certainly the case up on stage as Samsung unveiled its Galaxy S25 lineup of smartphones.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Communities walk tightrope as Trump ramps up immigration enforcement, threatens tariffs
item.description: The mayor El Paso and his chief of police on Wednesday said they will honor calls from federal law enforcement partners in need of assistance but will not volunteer municipal officers for immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Intoxicated mother, 2 kids found lying on freezing Texas sidewalk, sheriff's office says
item.description: A Texas mother is facing charges after she and her two young children were found lying outside as snow fell and temperatures dropped below freezing in Houston this week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nearly half of remote workers say they'd quit before returning to office full-time
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Nearly half of workers who currently work remotely at least some of the time would likely leave their jobs if ordered to return to the office full-time. At least, that's the key finding in a new survey from the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Judge Chutkan: Trump pardons won't change 'tragic truth' of Jan. 6 Capitol riot
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan, who oversaw President Trump's federal election subversion case, wrote in court filings that his sweeping pardons for those charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021 Capitol attack could not change the “tragic truth” of that day.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Vornado space heaters recalled over potential 'shock and fire' hazards
item.description: Thousands of Vornado space heaters sold on Amazon have been recalled amid concerns over possible "electric shock and fire hazards," according to the company.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has moved to end affirmative action in federal contracting and directed that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: ALEXANDRA OLSON and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: President Trump reacts to letter left for him by Biden
item.description: As part of an Inauguration Day tradition, the letter was left inside the Resolute Desk at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Cat mistakenly left in plane cargo hold makes 3 trips in 24 hours
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing risk of sending kids to school
item.description: Fears intensified for some when the Trump administration announced Tuesday it would allow federal immigration agencies to make arrests at schools, churches and hospitals, ending a decades-old policy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday issued an executive order directing the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to "return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring" and eliminate diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump demands apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: President Donald Trump early Wednesday morning slammed the reverend at a National Cathedral prayer service for the inauguration who called on him to have mercy on transgender children and immigrant families.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Elephants can't pursue their release from a Colorado zoo because they're not human, court says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Five elephants at a Colorado zoo may be “majestic” but, since they're not human, they do not have the legal right to pursue their release, Colorado’s highest court said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls out his blueprint on border security, but his orders will face challenges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump rolled out a blueprint to beef up security at the southern border in a series of executive orders that began taking effect soon after his inauguration Monday, making good on his defining political promise to crack down on immigration and marking another wild
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, GISELA SALOMON and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4
